text: In Aula 18 you told your career story: I have worked here for twelve years.
Today you use that story to answer interview questions and stay confident.

Tell me about yourself. Well, my background is in chemistry.
What have you done? I have managed a large sales team.
What is your strength? My biggest strength is communication.
What is your weakness? To be honest, I am too dedicated.
Why do you want this job? Because I love a challenge.
Welcome, Gleice. Thank you for coming. So, tell me about yourself.
Thank you. My background is in business, and I have worked in the commercial field for years.
Great. What have you done in your last position?
I have managed a large team, and my biggest strength is communication.
And your weakness? Please be honest.
To be honest, I am too dedicated. But I am confident I am the right candidate.
My background is in business.
I have managed a large team.
My biggest strength is communication.
To be honest, I am too dedicated.
I am a confident candidate for this job.

Hello, and welcome to the interview. Thank you for coming today. Let me ask you a few questions. First, tell me about yourself. I would like to hear about your background and your experience. Second, what have you done in your last position? Please give me one strong example. Third, what is your biggest strength, and what is one weakness you are working on? Be honest, please. And finally, why do you want this job, and what are your salary expectations? Take your time, stay calm, and stay confident. There are no wrong answers. I am very happy to meet a strong candidate like you today.

Hello, my name is Renata, and thank you for this interview. Let me tell you about myself. My background is in biology, and I have worked in the pharmaceutical field for ten years. In my last position, I have managed a large sales team. My biggest strength is communication, and I am a very confident and honest person. One weakness is that I am too dedicated, and sometimes I work too much. I have a good qualification for this role, and I really want this job because I love a challenge. My salary expectation is fair and open. I believe I am the right candidate for your team.

First, the interviewer welcomes the candidate and says thank you for coming. Then he asks the first question: tell me about yourself and your background. After that, he asks about a strength and a weakness, and the candidate is honest. Next, he asks why she wants the job and about her qualification. Finally, he asks about salary expectations and says she is a strong candidate.
